channel.title: NPR Topics: Science
channel.description: The latest health and science news. Updates on medicine, healthy living, nutrition, drugs, diet, and advances in science and technology. Subscribe to the Health & Science podcast.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: These roaches form exclusive long-term relationships after eating each other's wings
item.description: Salganea taiwanensis, a kind of wood-feeding cockroach, may engage in what's known as pair bonding, a new study finds.
item.content:encoded:
Salganea taiwanensis, a kind of wood-feeding cockroach, may engage in what's known as pair bonding, a new study finds.

(Image credit: Haruka Osaki)
item.dc:creator: Ari Daniel

item.title: Bringing marine life back to South Florida's 'forgotten edge'
item.description: Seawalls are great at protecting property and people. A new nature-inspired seawall add-on is trying to make them better at protecting marine wildlife too.
item.content:encoded:
Seawalls are great at protecting property and people. A new nature-inspired seawall add-on is trying to make them better at protecting marine wildlife too.

(Image credit: Nathan Rott)
item.dc:creator: Nathan Rott

item.title: Donations to science often avoid scrutiny. Jeffrey Epstein used this to his advantage
item.description: Billions of dollars are invested in science every year through philanthropy, with little legal or public scrutiny. Jeffrey Epstein utilized this to cultivate scientists and launder his reputation.
item.content:encoded: Billions of dollars are invested in science every year through philanthropy, with little legal or public scrutiny. Jeffrey Epstein utilized this to cultivate scientists and launder his reputation.
item.dc:creator: Katia Riddle

item.title: Why the driest place in North America is seeing an explosion of wildflowers
item.description: The driest place in North America is covered with an explosion of wildflowers right now. NPR's Ayesha Rascoe talks with ecologist Tiffany Pereira about Death Valley's superbloom.
item.content:encoded: The driest place in North America is covered with an explosion of wildflowers right now. NPR's Ayesha Rascoe talks with ecologist Tiffany Pereira about Death Valley's superbloom.
item.dc:creator: Ayesha Rascoe

item.title: Texas's state animals, armadillos, are making North Carolina their home
item.description: Armadillos are making North Carolina their home. NPR's Ayesha Rascoe talks with wildlife biologist Colleen Olfenbuttel about how Texas' state mammal has gotten a foothold in the Tar Heel State.
item.content:encoded: Armadillos are making North Carolina their home. NPR's Ayesha Rascoe talks with wildlife biologist Colleen Olfenbuttel about how Texas' state mammal has gotten a foothold in the Tar Heel State.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Benk

item.title: Master gardener Jessica Damiano explains seed packet lingo to aid successful gardening
item.description: Spring is just so close and with it - time to sow the seeds. But what do all those words on the packets really mean? NPR's Scott Simon talks with Master Gardener Jessica Damiano, columnist for the AP.
item.content:encoded: Spring is just so close and with it - time to sow the seeds. But what do all those words on the packets really mean? NPR's Scott Simon talks with Master Gardener Jessica Damiano, columnist for the AP.
item.dc:creator: Scott Simon

item.title: In layers of melting glacier ice, scientists find a story of pre-industrial pollution
item.description: A glacier in Europe holds clues about the activities of pre-industrial people, but it's melting fast.
item.content:encoded: A glacier in Europe holds clues about the activities of pre-industrial people, but it's melting fast.
item.dc:creator: Nell Greenfieldboyce

item.title: NASA targets Artemis II crewed moon mission for April 1 launch
item.description: A six-day launch window opens on April 1 from NASA's Kennedy Space Center in Florida. The lunar orbital mission would be the first time humans have returned to the moon since Apollo 17 in 1972.
item.content:encoded:
A six-day launch window opens on April 1 from NASA's Kennedy Space Center in Florida. The lunar orbital mission would be the first time humans have returned to the moon since Apollo 17 in 1972.

(Image credit: Gregg Newton)
item.dc:creator: Brendan Byrne

item.title: Easy-to-use solar panels are coming, but utilities are trying to delay them
item.description: Utilities are convincing lawmakers around the U.S. to delay bills that would allow people to buy solar panels, plug them into an outlet and begin generating electricity.
item.content:encoded:
Utilities are convincing lawmakers around the U.S. to delay bills that would allow people to buy solar panels, plug them into an outlet and begin generating electricity.

(Image credit: David J. Phillip)
item.dc:creator: Jeff Brady

item.title: 2025 saw relatively fewer natural disasters. Will you get a break on home insurance?
item.description: Disaster costs fell in the U.S. in 2025. Still, it was the fourth time in five years that extreme weather inflicted more than $100 billion in annual losses. Industry experts say the growing financial toll will make insurers wary of rushing to cut rates.
item.content:encoded:
Disaster costs fell in the U.S. in 2025. Still, it was the fourth time in five years that extreme weather inflicted more than $100 billion in annual losses. Industry experts say the growing financial toll will make insurers wary of rushing to cut rates.

(Image credit: ALLISON JOYCE/AFP via Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: Michael Copley
